claim: Recursive training on model-generated data can cause model collapse and loss of the true data distribution tails.
The Curse of Recursion paper argues that generated content in training can cause irreversible defects and tail disappearance.
Self-consuming generative model loops can degrade when synthetic data repeatedly feeds later generations.
Human or user curation can materially change outcomes in iterative retraining with generated data.
Genuine human interaction data becomes more valuable as online data contains more LLM-generated content.
